user.title: Add a Room
user.description: Configure Predictive Heating for a room. Rooms sharing the same climate entity are automatically grouped into a heating zone.

data.heat_share: Fraction of boiler heat going to this room

data_description.climate_entity: The thermostat that controls this room. If multiple rooms use the same thermostat, they will be grouped into a zone automatically.
data_description.gas_meter_sensor: Cumulative gas meter in m³. The integration computes its derivative to get the actual heating power in watts — much more accurate than treating heating as on/off. Later this can be replaced by a heat-pump electricity meter × COP.
data_description.boiler_efficiency: Seasonal boiler efficiency. HR-107 condensing boilers are typically 0.95; older boilers closer to 0.85.
data_description.heat_share: If you configure multiple rooms that share one gas meter, split the heat between them. Use 1.0 if only this room is modelled.
data_description.gas_calorific_value_mj_m3: Energy content per m³ of gas. Dutch Groningen-gas is ~35.17 MJ/m³; other grids differ.
data_description.floor_area_m2: Used once, to seed initial heat-loss and thermal-mass estimates. Leave blank to use generic defaults — the model will learn from observations regardless.
data_description.building_type: Roughly how well-insulated this room is. Used only for the initial bootstrap; the EKF will adjust H and C as it learns.
data_description.schedule_entity: Point to a schedule.* entity — the room target temperature will follow its ON/OFF state automatically. You can fine-tune the temperatures in the room's options.

data.heat_share: Fraction of boiler heat going to this room

data_description.room_name: Renaming a room keeps all learned thermal properties — only the displayed name changes.
data_description.max_setpoint_delta: How far the thermostat setpoint is allowed to drift above target. Small values (≤1°C) preserve OpenTherm modulation so the boiler/heat pump runs at the efficient flow temperature. Default 1.0°C.
data_description.nudge_step: How much to change the thermostat setpoint when a correction is needed. Defaults to 0.5°C — small enough to keep OpenTherm happy but noticeable.
data_description.nudge_interval_min: Minimum minutes between setpoint adjustments. Gives the boiler time to react before the next nudge.
data_description.gas_meter_sensor: Cumulative gas meter entity. Derivative → watts of heat delivered. Leave blank to fall back to binary heating on/off.
data_description.heat_share: When multiple rooms share one boiler, split the boiler heat between them (total should ≈ 1.0).
data_description.floor_area_m2: Used as an initial estimate for heat loss (H) and thermal mass (C). Only applied when the model hasn't learned anything yet.
data_description.building_type: Insulation quality category. Only affects the initial estimate — the EKF takes over once it has observations.
data_description.schedule_entity: Leave blank to set the temperature manually. When set, the room target follows this schedule entity's ON/OFF state. If the schedule slot defines a `temperature` attribute, that wins over the on/off defaults.
data_description.schedule_on_temp: Target temperature while the schedule entity is ON. Defaults to the Comfort temperature.
data_description.schedule_off_temp: Target temperature while the schedule entity is OFF. Defaults to the Eco temperature.
